title: Podman/Compose: "incorrect volume format" on Windows
body:
### Issue Description

> Error response from daemon: fill out specgen: C:\Users\vbespalov\Downloads\modules:/mnt/modules:rw: incorrect volume format, should be [host-dir:]ctr-dir[:option]

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```yaml
volumes:
  - ..:/mnt/modules
```


### Describe the results you received

Podman compose fails to parse the volume spec (Docker ok, btw)

### Describe the results you expected

The absolute path it substituted in the volume spec is parsed correctly

comments.body:
Long form also doesn't work:

```yaml
- type: bind
  source: ..
  target: /mnt/modules
```

Issues related to podman-compose should be reported [here](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose). Using native compose with podman is recommended because podman-compose hasn't implemented all features yet.

> Using native compose with podman is recommended

You mean "docker-compose"?

> You mean "docker-compose"?

Yes. Oh, I missed the windows part. No clue how this works in Windows. I used this for [macOS](https://gist.github.com/kaaquist/dab64aeb52a815b935b11c86202761a3). I think the process is the same.

I see. I tried both before opening the ticket: `docker-compose`, which was offered by Podman after installation, and `podman compose`, which, I assume, is a built-in script. Both yielded the same result.
I think, installing "real" Docker and using its `docker compose` could work, but on Windows there is no separate package with just CLI tools – only Docker Desktop is offered.

Compose does the replacement of relative path with absolute one automatically. There is no problem with `..` because I know it worked before – before Docker stopped working on my machine.
The problem is probably that the script parses the volume definition head-on using something like this:
```py
source, destination, mode = volume.split(":")
```

And it obviously doesn't work because there is a colon after the drive letter (`C:`) in the absolute path.

Thus:
> replace `..` with an absolute path

won't be of any help.

The problem is somewhere there:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/f526c37094fa2290fc6c18a375b55a7fd53689c4/pkg/specgen/volumes.go#L236-L237

`podman-compose` isn't the one to blame. I looked through its source code and did some testing – the bind mount argument is constructed properly and is passed to `podman` afterwards.

The error in the "Issue Description" is issued by `podman`:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/f526c37094fa2290fc6c18a375b55a7fd53689c4/pkg/specgen/volumes.go#L94

However, the relative path expansion is done by `podman-compose` in the first place:

https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/blob/a1f3befa8ea5c9aa0c84efb9c1f7a267c6cc0c1a/podman_compose.py#L171

No, Windows paths are handled correctly in `SplitVolumeString`. There are tests, even.
The only possibility of what may have went wrong is that `shouldResolveWinPaths()` returned `false` on Windows.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/f526c37094fa2290fc6c18a375b55a7fd53689c4/pkg/specgen/volumes.go#L238-L240

I also don't see any build constraints defined at the top of `winpath_linux.go` and `winpath_windows.go`:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/fac259ef9c45d45597f9ae554b221fc5b55935b6/pkg/specgen/winpath_linux.go#L1
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/fac259ef9c45d45597f9ae554b221fc5b55935b6/pkg/specgen/winpath_windows.go#L1

As opposed to `winpath_unsupported.go`:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/fac259ef9c45d45597f9ae554b221fc5b55935b6/pkg/specgen/winpath_unsupported.go#L1

(That's where `shouldResolveWinPaths` is defined)
